headline: Starbucks Builds Sovereign AI to Cut $400 Million in Software Costs
summary: Starbucks (SBUX) is building internal AI tools to replace Microsoft and IBM software, targeting $400 million in annual vendor spend and cutting $30 million from its tech budget.

headline: Is Palo Alto Networks' (PANW) AI Cost Push Reframing Its Cybersecurity Platform Ambitions?
summary: In recent days, Palo Alto Networks has highlighted expanded AI-driven cybersecurity initiatives, including new collaborations with IBM, Red Hat and Tenzai, along with the launch of its Idira identity security platform and continued growth in platformized customers. CEO Nikesh Arora’s public push for sharply lower AI token costs underscores both the economic hurdles to enterprise-scale AI adoption and Palo Alto Networks’ ambition to sit at the center of AI-powered security. We’ll now consider...

headline: IBM (IBM) Launches Lightwell And Expands Bob For Enterprise Security And Modernization
summary: International Business Machines (NYSE:IBM) commercially launched Lightwell with Red Hat, an AI-driven platform for automated remediation of vulnerabilities in open source software dependencies. IBM also released a major update to its Bob agentic software development platform, adding AI-native modernization workflows for mainframe, IBM i, and Java environments. International Business Machines enters this news cycle with its stock at $287.56 and a 5.6% gain over the past 30 days. The...
